chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Hi guys, welcome to another live session by SimpleLearn. I would like to thank you all for actively participating in these sessions on YouTube and Facebook. Today, we will understand how to perform stock market prediction using machine learning. Now let's wait for a while for people to join in.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: We have a really exciting session today. Meanwhile, please subscribe to our SimpleLearn YouTube channel and stay updated with all the latest trending technologies. Also, if you have any questions for us during the course of this video, please put it in the chat section.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Our team of experts will help you solve your queries. I can see a lot of people have joined in without any further delay. Let's begin. So, what really is stock market? A stock market is a place or a public market where you can buy and sell shares for publicly

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: listed companies. The stocks which are also known as equities represent ownership in the company. The stock exchange is the mediator that allows the buying and selling of shares. Amsterdam stock exchange is the oldest stock exchange that were established in 1602. The top stock exchanges in the United States are the New York Stock Exchange, NASDAQ, American

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Stock Exchange and Chicago Board Operations Exchange. In India, we have the Bombay Stock Exchange or the BSC that was established in 1875 and its ACS first stock exchange. We also have the National Stock Exchange or NSE. Now, let's understand the importance of stock market.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Stock markets help companies to raise capital. If stock markets did not exist, then companies would have to resort to borrowing loans from the banks to raise money for expansion. This would be a trouble for the company as they would have to repay the loans with interest. With stock markets, companies have the free hand to create an initial public offering and

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: raise large amounts of cash without having to worry about repayment. Another reason why the stock market is important is it helps generate personal wealth. For an individual investor, the stock market provides a platform to invest your income and earn a share of the company's profit. Stock markets serve as an indicator of the state of the economy, that is, the stock market

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: serves as a barometer for the economy. A rise of fall in the prices of shares indicates what cycle the economy is in, such as a recession or a boom. The stock market is considered to be one of the most widely used sources for people to invest money in.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Investors are always looking to invest in companies with high growth potential. If the stock market is performing well, this increases investment from local investors. It also attracts foreign direct investment as people abroad invest in the local stock exchange. With that, let's now understand stock market prediction.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Stock market prediction helps you determine the future value of company stock and other financial instruments traded on an exchange. The whole idea of predicting stock prices is to gain significant profits. Predicting how the stock market will perform is a difficult task to do. There are a lot of other factors involved in the prediction such as physical and psychological

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: factors, rational and irrational behaviour, etc. All these factors combine to make share prices dynamic and volatile. This makes it very difficult to predict stock prices with high accuracy. So we will use machine learning techniques to predict the stock prices of two different companies, Tesla and Google.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: We will be using linear regression algorithm and long short term memory networks for building our prediction models. So first, let's understand the basics of linear regression. Linear regression is a very popular statistical technique used for solving machine learning problems.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: It is a supervised learning algorithm for predicting the output of a continuous target variable. It can be used to predict the total revenue of a company or the total units expected to be sold of a certain product. It can also be used for weather prediction and stock price prediction.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Here is an example to predict the total number of ice creams a vendor or a company can sell based on the temperature of the day. You can see the green dots represent the actual data points. So the x-axis has the input variable that is temperature and the y-axis has the target variable that is unit sold.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Linear regression uses the linear equation formula y is equal to mx plus c where m is the slope of the line, c is the intercept. We find the coefficients and plot the regression line. So in this graph, the red line is a regression line. The best fit line should have the least squared distance between the original data points and

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: the predicted values. Now let's see how to predict the stock prices of Tesla using the linear regression model. So this is the csv data set that we will be using for our prediction. We have information from 29 June 2010 till 15 May 2019. Here open column means the price at which a stock started trading when the market opened

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: on a particular day. The close column refers to the price of an individual stock when the stock exchange closed market for the day. It represents the last buy sell order executed between two traders. The high column is the highest price at which a stock traded during a period.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: The low is the lowest price of the period. Same is the total amount of trading activity during a period of time. The adjusted closing price is a calculation adjustment made to the stock's closing price. It is more complex and accurate than the closing price. The adjustment made to the closing price depicts the true price of the stock because the outside

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: factors could have altered the true price. Now before I begin the demo, I want to tell you that if you want to get a copy of the source code files and the data sets that I will be using in this session, then please put your email IDs in the chat section of the video. Also, please subscribe to our channel and hit the bell icon to stay updated with all

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: the latest trending technologies. So here is my Jupyter notebook where I have implemented my linear regression code. So I will run through each cell of code and explain what it does. First, we will import the necessary libraries for building our model. So I am importing pandas, numpy and macplotlib for numerical computation, data manipulation

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: and data visualization. We will also import the Plotly library for building our graphs. Now if you are using Plotly for the first time, you will have to install it and then import it onto Jupyter. Let me run this cell.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: So I have successfully imported all my libraries. Next, I load the tesla.stoprise data set using the pandas library and the read underscore cse function. I will also give the location where my csv data set is present. Let me import the data set.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: I will hit shift enter. So now I have successfully imported my data set. Now let's see my data set. For that I will use the head function, I will load tesla.head. So this will return the top 5 rows from the data set.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: In the next cell, I am using the info function to find the total rows and columns in my data set. The info function will also return the data type of the variables and check if there are any null values. The UO.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: So we have total 7 columns and there are 2193 entries. Here you can see the data type of each column and each column has non null values. Moving ahead, in the next step, we are converting the date column into date time format using pandas library and 2 underscore date time function. Up next, let's do some exploratory data analysis and get more insights from our data.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: I am using the print function and a formatted string literal that is f. These strings contain replacement fields which are expressions delimited by curly braces. You can see here, I am finding out the data frame contains top prices between what dates and the total number of dates. So the total number of days can be calculated by subtracting the maximum date value and

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: the minimum date value. So let me go ahead and run it. You can see stock prices are from 29th of June 2010 till 15th of March 2019 and the total number of days are 3181 days. Now let's see some summary statistics for our data using the describe function.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: So the describe function gives you the count, mean, standard deviation, the maximum and minimum from each column, the 25th quartile, the 50th quartile and the 75th quartile. Let me now go ahead and create a box plot to visually check our outliers. I am considering 5 columns, open, high, low, close and adjusted clues. So this is how our box plot looks like.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: In this cell, we will plot a graph using the plotly library that we have imported. We are setting the layout for our graph using the go layout function and providing a title to the graph. I am also setting my x-axis for giving the title. So the x-axis will have the date column you can see it here.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: We are also defining the title font, the family, size and color. Similarly, I am providing the layout for the y-axis you can see it here. Finally, I am passing my data list and the layout created to plot variable. Let me go ahead and run it. Now I will plot my graph using the iplot function.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: There you go. So we have the stock prices of Tesla plotted in this graph. On x-axis, we have the year from 2011 to 2019 and on the y-axis, you have the price which is basically the close column. You can see from the graph that initially during 2011 or 2010, the stock prices were

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: growing a little lower than expected. But towards 2015, 16 and 17 onwards, the stock prices grew higher. Now coming to the most important part, that is to build the linear regression model. So in this cell, I am importing the necessary libraries and their respective functions. First, I am importing the train underscore test underscore split function from the scikit

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: learn library. Next, I am also importing some pre-processing functions like minmax scalar and standard scalar from scikit learn library. Also, for evaluating the model and finding the accuracy, we will use the mean squared error and r squared error from scikit learn matrix library.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Let me run it. So we have successfully imported our required libraries. Moving ahead, in the current step, we are splitting the data into training and testing sets. The x variable contains the independent features and the y variable has the dependent variable or the target variable that is close column.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: I am taking the test data sizes 0.3 or 30% of the total and I am assigning a random state of 101. Let's run it. Okay. Now I will perform feature scaling on the extrained dataset.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: For that, I am using the standard scalar dot fit function and passing in my extrained data. The standardization of a dataset is a common requirement for any machine learning algorithm. They might behave badly if the individual features are not normally distributed. So that's why feature scaling is important. With that, it's time for us to import the linear regression function from the scikit

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: learn library. Now using a variable lm which stands for linear model, I will declare a linear regression function and then I will use the fit method to pass the training datasets that is x train and y train. So let me run it.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Now we have successfully created a linear regression model. Next we will plot the graph for actual and predicted values for the training dataset. Here we will be creating a scatterplot and draw our prediction line. So I have used the go.scatter function. I am using markers for plotting the actual data points and I have used a line to draw

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: the prediction line. Let me run it. Now to plot the graph, I used the iplot function. If I scroll down, you can see on the x axis, we have the day column or the day variable and on the y axis, we have the prices of our stocks.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: The blue dots represent the actual stock values. If I hover over these blue dots, you can see the actual values and the red line is a predicted regression line. The final step will calculate the scores for evaluating our model. So here we are using two matrix.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: One is r squared error and mean squared error. For finding the r squared error, we have used r2 underscore score function and I am passing my y train as well as x train using the lm predict function. Similarly, for finding the r squared error for testing dataset, I am using y test and lm predict for x test.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Likewise to find the mean squared error value, I have used the y train and y test data along with predicted x train and x test data. Now let me run it. Below you can see the two matrix scores. So our r squared error score is around 0.86 for the training set and it is around the same

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: value of 0.86 for testing dataset and similarly you can see the mean squared error as well. With that, you saw how to predict the tesla stock prices using linear regression model in Python. If you want to get a copy of the source code files and the datasets that I am using in this session, then please put your email IDs in the chat section.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: We will share the files over email. Also please subscribe to our channel and stay updated with all the latest technologies. Now we will understand about long short term memory networks. So LSTMs are a type of recurrent neural networks for learning long term dependencies. It is commonly used for processing and predicting on the basis of time series data.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: From the image on the left, you can see LSTMs have a chain like structure. Instead of having a single neural network layer, there are four interacting layers communicating with each other in a very special way. Now LSTMs work in a three step process. The first step in LSTM is to decide which information to be omitted from the cell in

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: that particular time step. It is decided by the help of a sigmoid function. It looks at the previous state that is h t minus 1 and the current input state that is x t and computes the function. In the second layer, there are two parts.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: One is the sigmoid function and the other is the tanh function or the hyperbolic tangent function. In the sigmoid function, it decides which values to let through, that is 0 or 1. The hyperbolic tangent function gives the weightage to the values which are passed deciding their level of importance from minus 1 to plus 1.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: In the third step, it decides what will be your final output. First, it will run a sigmoid layer which decides which parts of the cell state make it to the output. Then we put the cell state through the tanh function to push the values to be between minus 1 and plus 1 and multiply it by the output of the sigmoid gate.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Now, let's go to our Jupyter notebook and implement a long short term memory network to predict the stock prices of Google. Before that, let me first show you the data set that we will be using. So this is my Jupyter lab and on the left, you can see we have our Google data sets. So we will be using two data sets for this demo, a training data set and a testing data

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: set. Let me show you how the training data set looks like. I'll open the CSV table. So this is our training data set of Google. It has the same columns like date, open, high, low, close and volume.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: And if I scroll down, you can see we have data from 2012 till 2016. We close this. Let me open my Google test data set. So this is my test data set and we'll be using this data set to evaluate our model. So first, let me go ahead and import all the libraries that will be needing to build

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: our LSTM model. You can see I'm importing the num p pandas and matplotlib library for numerical computation, data manipulation and data visualization. We are also importing minmax scalar function from scikit-learn preprocessing library. Then we have some built-in functions from the Keras library, such as sequential to analyze

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: a sequence of data, then dense LSTM and drop out to measure the dropout rate. I'll hit shift enter to run this cell. You can see it says we are using TensorFlow backend. Now let me go ahead and import the Google training data set using read underscore CSV function from pandas.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: I have imported my data set. Now if you want to check the head of the data set, you can use data.head. Run again. So it will display the top five rows from the data set. Similarly, now let's see the total rows and columns we have in this data set and the

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: data types of each variable. We'll use the info function for this. You can see this is my output. So we have a total of six columns and 1258 entries. And here you can see the data type.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: So date it has taken as object, open it has taken as float, high it has taken as float, similarly close and volume column it has taken as object. Now we will do some pre-processing of data to transform our closing price variable to a numeric type. Drop the non-available data and keep only the close column for training the data.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Now to convert the close column into numeric, I have used PD dot to underscore numeric function. Now to drop my missing values, I have used drop any function. And similarly, to consider only closing column as my training data, I'm using i-lock or index location. Now let me run it.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Now let's once again see the data types. Scroll down. You can see we have changed the close column data type to float. Earlier it was object. Now it's float.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Next, we will rescale our data into values between zero and one for better performances. Now that you can use the minmax scalar function and give the range. Then I have used fit underscore transform method to transform the values. And then I'm also printing the shape of my training data. Let me run it.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Now we will prepare our data for our training. First, we need to prepare our input sequences with 60 time steps of X train data alongside the respective Y train labels. Next, we'll be adding another axis for the batch size since the input for an LSTM network is a 3D tensor.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: So it will have a sequence length, the time steps and batch size. Now, we'll build a model containing four layers of LSTM network all followed by a dropout layer. On the top, we'll also have a final dense layer which is this one. All of this compiled using an atom optimizer and a mean squared error as a loss function.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Let me run it. Next, I'll train the data using 20 epochs and batch size of 32 using the model dot fit function and passing my X train and Y train data. This will take some time to run since it will complete 20 iterations. So you can see it has started with first epoch and it will run for 20 epochs.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Now we are into our second epoch. You can see the loss that was incurred during the training of the first epoch. Meanwhile, when our model is getting trained, if you have any questions, please put it in the chat section. You can see we have completed our fifth epoch as well.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Now we are into our seventh epoch. Now we have entered our fifteenth epoch. Now we are almost about to finish our training. We have entered into our final epoch now. I think we have successfully completed training our model.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Next, let's visualize the loss that was incurred during the training process for each epoch. I am using the matplotlib library and the plot function. I have given a title to my visualization. I have also defined my X labels and Y labels. So below you can see this is how the graph looks like.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: On the top you can see the title that is training model loss. You can see the loss has been a steep decline. With that, let's test the model using a new dataset. We will continue with the same steps of inputting the dataset. So you can see I am using the read underscore csv function to import my test Google dataset.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Then we will convert the closed column into a numeric type. I will then drop the missing values and select the closing price columns for testing. Next, I am selecting the labels for Y test data. Finally, we are providing the input array for the model and converting the X test data into a numpy array and printing its shape.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Let me hit shift enter to run it. In the next cell, I am predicting the model output using the model dot predict function and passing the X test data. So let me run it. Now if you want to check the values of Y prediction,

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: you can just give the variable name and hit shift enter. So these are my values. It's not in a proper order or a proper format. Now, to plot the data between actual and predicted stock prices, we will use the inverse underscore transform function over Y-Pred data.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: And finally, we will plot the graph in order to visualize the actual stock price and the predicted stock price. I am using the Macplotlib library and I will pass in the data. We are also providing a title to the plot, the X labels and the Y labels. So this is how the graph looks like. On the X axis, you have time and on the Y axis, you have the stock price.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: The red line indicates actual stock price, while the green line indicates the predicted stock price. With that, we have successfully created our model and predicted the stock price values using long short term memory networks. If you have any questions, please put it in the chat section. We'll be happy to help you. Also, if you want to get the source code files and datasets I have used for linear regression and LSTM,

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: then please put your email address in the chat section. We'll send it to you over email. With that, we have reached the end of this live session. I hope it was useful and informative. If you liked the video, then please consider subscribing to our YouTube channel and hit the bell icon to never miss an update from Simply Learn.

chunks.topic: Stock Market Prediction Using Machine Learning
chunks.text: Thank you for watching. Stay safe and keep learning.

text: Hi guys, welcome to another live session by SimpleLearn. I would like to thank you all for actively participating in these sessions on YouTube and Facebook. Today, we will understand how to perform stock market prediction using machine learning. Now let's wait for a while for people to join in. We have a really exciting session today. Meanwhile, please subscribe to our SimpleLearn YouTube channel and stay updated with all the latest trending technologies. Also, if you have any questions for us during the course of this video, please put it in the chat section. Our team of experts will help you solve your queries. I can see a lot of people have joined in without any further delay. Let's begin. So, what really is stock market? A stock market is a place or a public market where you can buy and sell shares for publicly listed companies. The stocks which are also known as equities represent ownership in the company. The stock exchange is the mediator that allows the buying and selling of shares. Amsterdam stock exchange is the oldest stock exchange that were established in 1602. The top stock exchanges in the United States are the New York Stock Exchange, NASDAQ, American Stock Exchange and Chicago Board Operations Exchange. In India, we have the Bombay Stock Exchange or the BSC that was established in 1875 and its ACS first stock exchange. We also have the National Stock Exchange or NSE. Now, let's understand the importance of stock market. Stock markets help companies to raise capital. If stock markets did not exist, then companies would have to resort to borrowing loans from the banks to raise money for expansion. This would be a trouble for the company as they would have to repay the loans with interest. With stock markets, companies have the free hand to create an initial public offering and raise large amounts of cash without having to worry about repayment. Another reason why the stock market is important is it helps generate personal wealth. For an individual investor, the stock market provides a platform to invest your income and earn a share of the company's profit. Stock markets serve as an indicator of the state of the economy, that is, the stock market serves as a barometer for the economy. A rise of fall in the prices of shares indicates what cycle the economy is in, such as a recession or a boom. The stock market is considered to be one of the most widely used sources for people to invest money in. Investors are always looking to invest in companies with high growth potential. If the stock market is performing well, this increases investment from local investors. It also attracts foreign direct investment as people abroad invest in the local stock exchange. With that, let's now understand stock market prediction. Stock market prediction helps you determine the future value of company stock and other financial instruments traded on an exchange. The whole idea of predicting stock prices is to gain significant profits. Predicting how the stock market will perform is a difficult task to do. There are a lot of other factors involved in the prediction such as physical and psychological factors, rational and irrational behaviour, etc. All these factors combine to make share prices dynamic and volatile. This makes it very difficult to predict stock prices with high accuracy. So we will use machine learning techniques to predict the stock prices of two different companies, Tesla and Google. We will be using linear regression algorithm and long short term memory networks for building our prediction models. So first, let's understand the basics of linear regression. Linear regression is a very popular statistical technique used for solving machine learning problems. It is a supervised learning algorithm for predicting the output of a continuous target variable. It can be used to predict the total revenue of a company or the total units expected to be sold of a certain product. It can also be used for weather prediction and stock price prediction. Here is an example to predict the total number of ice creams a vendor or a company can sell based on the temperature of the day. You can see the green dots represent the actual data points. So the x-axis has the input variable that is temperature and the y-axis has the target variable that is unit sold. Linear regression uses the linear equation formula y is equal to mx plus c where m is the slope of the line, c is the intercept. We find the coefficients and plot the regression line. So in this graph, the red line is a regression line. The best fit line should have the least squared distance between the original data points and the predicted values. Now let's see how to predict the stock prices of Tesla using the linear regression model. So this is the csv data set that we will be using for our prediction. We have information from 29 June 2010 till 15 May 2019. Here open column means the price at which a stock started trading when the market opened on a particular day. The close column refers to the price of an individual stock when the stock exchange closed market for the day. It represents the last buy sell order executed between two traders. The high column is the highest price at which a stock traded during a period. The low is the lowest price of the period. Same is the total amount of trading activity during a period of time. The adjusted closing price is a calculation adjustment made to the stock's closing price. It is more complex and accurate than the closing price. The adjustment made to the closing price depicts the true price of the stock because the outside factors could have altered the true price. We will also import the Plotly library for building our graphs. Now if you are using Plotly for the first time, you will have to install it and then import it onto Jupyter. Let me run this cell. In the next cell, I am using the info function to find the total rows and columns in my data set. The info function will also return the data type of the variables and check if there are any null values. The UO. So we have total 7 columns and there are 2193 entries. Here you can see the data type of each column and each column has non null values. Moving ahead, in the next step, we are converting the date column into date time format using pandas library and 2 underscore date time function. Up next, let's do some exploratory data analysis and get more insights from our data. I am using the print function and a formatted string literal that is f. These strings contain replacement fields which are expressions delimited by curly braces. You can see here, I am finding out the data frame contains top prices between what dates and the total number of dates. So the total number of days can be calculated by subtracting the maximum date value and the minimum date value. So let me go ahead and run it. So LSTMs are a type of recurrent neural networks for learning long term dependencies. It is commonly used for processing and predicting on the basis of time series data. From the image on the left, you can see LSTMs have a chain like structure. Instead of having a single neural network layer, there are four interacting layers communicating with each other in a very special way. Now LSTMs work in a three step process. The first step in LSTM is to decide which information to be omitted from the cell in that particular time step. It is decided by the help of a sigmoid function. It looks at the previous state that is h t minus 1 and the current input state that is x t and computes the function. In the second layer, there are two parts. One is the sigmoid function and the other is the tanh function or the hyperbolic tangent function. In the sigmoid function, it decides which values to let through, that is 0 or 1. The hyperbolic tangent function gives the weightage to the values which are passed deciding their level of importance from minus 1 to plus 1. In the third step, it decides what will be your final output. First, it will run a sigmoid layer which decides which parts of the cell state make it to the output. Then we put the cell state through the tanh function to push the values to be between minus 1 and plus 1 and multiply it by the output of the sigmoid gate.
